title: Ground Turkey and Sweet Potato Bowls
metaDescription: Ground turkey and sweet potato meal prep bowls with 34g of protein per container — one pan, five servings, ready in 35 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: ground turkey meal prep, turkey sweet potato bowls, high protein meal prep

steps.name: Roast the sweet potato
steps.text: Toss cubed sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast at 425°F for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until fork-tender.

steps.name: Cook the turkey
steps.text: Heat remaining olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey, smoked paprika, garlic powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Cook 8-10 minutes, breaking it up as it browns, until fully cooked through.

steps.name: Steam the green beans
steps.text: Steam or blanch green beans for 4-5 minutes until bright green and just tender.

steps.name: Assemble containers
steps.text: Divide turkey, sweet potato, and green beans evenly between 5 containers. Cool completely before sealing and refrigerating.

tips.question: Can I use ground chicken instead of turkey?
tips.answer: Yes, ground chicken works as a direct 1:1 swap with the same cook time and seasoning.

tips.question: How long do these bowls keep in the fridge?
tips.answer: Up to 5 days when cooled completely before sealing. Reheat only the portion you're eating.

tips.question: Why does my ground turkey turn out dry?
tips.answer: Overcooking past fully-browned is the main cause — 93% lean turkey has less fat than beef, so pull it off the heat as soon as no pink remains rather than cooking further for extra browning.

tips.question: Can I swap the sweet potato for regular potato?
tips.answer: Yes, though cook time may vary slightly — check for fork-tenderness rather than relying on the exact time listed.

images.heroAlt: Meal prep containers with seasoned ground turkey, roasted sweet potato cubes and green beans
images.textureAlt: Seasoned crumbled ground turkey next to a cut roasted sweet potato cube
images.pinAlt: Fork lifting seasoned ground turkey and roasted sweet potato cubes from a meal prep container

storyIntro: Ground turkey gets a bad reputation for turning dry and bland the moment it's reheated, but that's usually a seasoning and timing problem, not a turkey problem. This version leans on smoked paprika, cumin, and garlic to fix both.
Sweet potato and green beans round it out into a genuinely balanced bowl — 34g of protein, five containers, ready in 35 minutes of mostly hands-off cooking.
storyNote: Pull the turkey off the heat the moment no pink remains. 93% lean turkey has very little fat to spare, so any extra time on the stove chasing more browning is what dries it out.
